statement.p: If both Pipe A and Pipe B are turned on, it would take 4 hours to fill a tank. If each pipe is turned on alone, it takes Pipe B 6 fewer hours than Pipe A to fill the tank. Answer the following questions:
If only Pipe A is turned on, it would take hours to fill the tank.
If only Pipe B is turned on, it would take hours to fill the tank.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a pipe 5 hours to fill a tank. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, the pipe would fill \frac{1}{5} of the tank.
Similarly, if it takes a pipe x hours to fill a tank, each hour, the pipe would fill \frac{1}{x} of the tank.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Assume that it would take Pipe A x hours to fill the tank. Each hour, Pipe A fills \frac{1}{x} of the tank.
Since it takes Pipe B 6 fewer hours than Pipe A to fill the tank, it would take Pipe B x-6 hours to fill the tank (Pipe B is faster!). Each hour, Pipe B fills \frac{1}{x-6} of the tank.
Its given that, if both pipes are turned on, it would take 4 hours to fill the tank. Each hour, they can fill \frac{1}{4} of the tank.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} \amp \frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{x-6} = \frac{1}{4} \\ \amp \frac{1}{x} \cdot {4x\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)} +\frac{1}{x-6} \cdot {4x\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)} = \frac{1}{4} \cdot {4x\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)} \\ \amp 4(x-6) + 4x = x(x-6) \\ \amp 4x - 24 +4x = x^{2}-6x \\ \amp 8x - 24 = x^{2}-6x \\ \amp 0= x^{2}-6x -8x + 24 \\ \amp 0= x^{2} - 14x + 24 \\ \amp 0= (x-12)(x-2) \\ \amp x-12=0 \:\: or\:\: x-2=0 \\ \amp x=12 \:\: or\:\: x=2 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {4x\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)}.
Since it takes Pipe B 6 fewer hours than Pipe A to fill the tank, it would take Pipe B {12}-6={6} hours or 2-6=-4 hours to fill the tank
In this situation, -4 is not a valid solution for Pipe B, since hours cannot be negative. x=12 is the only solution. Therefore, it takes Pipe A {12} hours and Pipe B {6} hours to fill the tank.
